failure_samples.summary: doAssert is NOT compiled out in -d:danger mode on Nim 2.3.1
failure_samples.evidence: nim c -d:danger test_c12_doassert_danger.nim → AssertionDefect raised, proving doAssert still executes

corrections.correction: doAssert is NOT compiled out in -d:danger on Nim 2.3.1. It raises AssertionDefect in all build modes. Plain assert IS compiled out in -d:danger.
assert is compiled out in -d:danger (silently skipped). assert fires in default and -d:release modes. doAssert fires in all modes including danger.

claims.claim_text: doAssert raises AssertionDefect on failure and the process exits with a non-zero code
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. doAssert raises AssertionDefect with the message string.

claims.claim_text: block scopes isolate test names so multiple blocks can reuse local variable names
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Three blocks each declaring `let x` or `var sum` compile and run without conflict.

claims.claim_text: -d:danger disables overflow checks: OverflowDefect is not raised for integer overflow
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. In danger mode, high(int)-1 + 2 wraps without raising. In default/release mode it raises OverflowDefect.

claims.claim_text: Default and -d:release modes raise OverflowDefect for integer overflow
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Both default and -d:release raise OverflowDefect.

claims.claim_text: switch("path", "$projectdir/../src") in tests/config.nims resolves imports from src/ for any .nim file in tests/
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. testlib imported successfully via config.nims path switch.

claims.claim_text: The auto-discovering tester.nim walks tests/t*.nim, skips tester.nim and thelper.nim, and runs each discovered file via nim c -r
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. walkFiles with glob pattern works correctly; also validated end-to-end in /tmp project.

claims.claim_text: execShellCmd returns non-zero on test failure, and the runner calls quit with exit code 1
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. check/summary pattern correctly counts failures and quit exits with code 1.

claims.claim_text: Default mode shows full stack traces with file path and line number
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. getStackTrace() returns non-empty trace in default mode.

claims.claim_text: -d:release shows only the raising frame in stack traces
claims.evaluation_notes: Not independently tested; aligned with nim-debugging skill verification which confirmed this behavior.

claims.claim_text: -d:danger shows only the raising frame in stack traces
claims.evaluation_notes: Not independently tested; aligned with nim-debugging skill verification which confirmed this behavior.

claims.claim_text: --stackTrace:on --lineTrace:on restores full stack traces in release and danger modes
claims.evaluation_notes: Not independently tested; aligned with nim-debugging skill verification which confirmed this behavior.

claims.claim_text: doAssert is compiled out in -d:danger mode
claims.evaluation_notes: INCORRECT. doAssert still executes in -d:danger on Nim 2.3.1. The test `doAssert false, '...'` raised AssertionDefect in danger mode. Claim is disproven. Plain assert IS compiled out in danger (see C18).

claims.claim_text: The expected-failure pattern (try/except with a boolean flag and doAssert) correctly catches raised exceptions
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. try/except ValueError pattern catches the exception and doAssert confirms the flag.

claims.claim_text: The check/summary helper pattern counts passes and failures, and quit with exit code 1 on any failure
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Verified in C07 and C14 independently.

claims.claim_text: AddressSanitizer with --passC/--passL, -g, -d:noSignalHandler, -d:useMalloc detects use-after-free and reports Nim file and line number
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. ASan detected heap-use-after-free with Nim source file and line number in the report.

claims.claim_text: -d:useMalloc makes Nim use C malloc instead of its own allocator
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Compiles and runs with -d:useMalloc. ASan interoperation confirmed in C15.

claims.claim_text: The addressSanitizer define in tests/config.nims activates sanitizer flags via config instead of command line
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. `nim c -d:addressSanitizer -r test_c15_asan_detect.nim` triggered ASan via config.nims switches.

claims.claim_text: assert is compiled out in -d:danger (silently skipped) but fires in default and -d:release; doAssert fires in all modes
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Verified across all three modes: assert fires in default/release, compiled out in danger. doAssert fires in all modes.

claims.claim_text: AssertionDefect (from doAssert) inherits from Defect, not CatchableError. Bare except: and except CatchableError do not catch it. Use except AssertionDefect, except Defect, or except Exception.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Bare except: does not catch AssertionDefect. Negative test (test_c19_defect_not_caught_bad.nim) confirms: the test itself fails because except: doesn't catch the Defect.
